title: Rottlera tanarius
taxonomy: Rottlera tanarius is classified within the family Celastraceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the staff vine family. This family consists of about 60-70 genera and 1,000 species, with Rottlera tanarius being one of the species within the genus Rottlera. The genus Rottlera itself is native to tropical Asia, and Rottlera tanarius is specifically found in Southeast Asia. The plant is a type of evergreen shrub or small tree, characterized by its distinctive leaves and flowers. Classification of Rottlera tanarius is further divided into its binomial nomenclature, with the species name tanarius providing more specific details about the plant's characteristics.
uses: Rottlera tanarius, also known as the 'Indian staff tree', has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's roots and bark are said to possess antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in treating wounds and reducing swelling. In Ayurvedic medicine, Rottlera tanarius is believed to have antipyretic properties, helping to reduce fever. The plant is also used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant properties. Furthermore, Rottlera tanarius is thought to have antimicrobial properties, making it effective against various bacterial and fungal infections.

Its leaves are said to have anti-diarrheal and anti-ulcer properties, making it useful in treating digestive issues. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. These active constituents are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. Flavonoids, in particular, have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. intro: Rottlera tanarius, also known as Rottlera, is a plant species belonging to the family Celastraceae, commonly found in various parts of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Rottlera tanarius include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
